content.body: At the end of https://tc39.es/ecma262/#sec-asyncblockstart, there's a Note about the possible sources of `_result_`. What I don't understand is, if it's coming from Await, how do you know it's `~unused~`? (Seems more like it would have to be an ES lang value.)
content.formatted_body: At the end of https://tc39.es/ecma262/#sec-asyncblockstart, there's a Note about the possible sources of _result_. What I don't understand is, if it's coming from Await, how do you know it's ~unused~? (Seems more like it would have to be an ES lang value.)

content.body: but by "coming from Await", it means coming from (what is now) RunCallerContext step 4
which Await invokes in its step 8, passing `empty` https://tc39.es/ecma262/multipage/control-abstraction-objects.html#await
content.formatted_body: which Await invokes in its step 8, passing empty https://tc39.es/ecma262/multipage/control-abstraction-objects.html#await

content.body: I think Await should probably be passing `unused` there
content.formatted_body: I think Await should probably be passing unused there

content.body: it used to in the 2022 edition, got changed sometime between then and 2023, let me see
what I mean is, https://tc39.es/ecma262/2022/multipage/ecmascript-data-types-and-values.html#await step 10 is `unused`
content.formatted_body: what I mean is, https://tc39.es/ecma262/2022/multipage/ecmascript-data-types-and-values.html#await step 10 is unused
